name: Olympians
shortDescription: The Olympians are a group of twelve major deities in Greek mythology who resided on Mount Olympus. This concept is sometimes used comparatively when discussing Norse deities.
description: The term 'Olympians' refers specifically to the principal gods and goddesses in the Greek pantheon who were believed to reside on Mount Olympus. While not directly part of Norse mythology, the concept of a ruling family or council of gods is a common motif across many polytheistic religions, including Norse paganism. Therefore, 'Olympians' can serve as a point of comparison when discussing the Aesir, the principal group of deities in Norse belief. Just as the Olympians were central to Greek religious life and cultural identity, the Aesir held a similar position of prominence in Norse society. The use of 'Olympians' in a Norse context highlights the shared human tendency to organize deities into hierarchical structures with specific roles and responsibilities. This comparison allows for exploration of the differing approaches to divine governance, cosmology, and the relationship between gods and mortals in different cultures. While the term itself is foreign to Norse mythology, the underlying concept of a powerful, resident group of deities resonates with the structure and function of the Aesir in Asgard. Understanding the concept of the Olympians aids in cross-cultural analysis of religious beliefs and mythological narratives.

sources.title: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe
sources.author: H.R. Ellis Davidson
